title: Attention, Amazon Haters: Walmart Is Also Doing a Big Sale Right Now
author: Simon Hill
text: All products featured on WIRED are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links. Learn more.Prime Day is in full swing, but Amazon isn't the only shop with deals. Walmart has a competing sale it calls, in a burst of creativity, “Walmart Deals.” Like Prime Day, Walmart's sale features a range of deals on headphones, tablets, kitchen gear, and other tech. The best part is, there are no members-only deals here, so you can shop without worrying you'll be asked to sign up for one. I've rounded up all the best Walmart deals on our favorite WIRED-tested gear.Shop Walmart's Summer Sale HereIf you can't find anything you like, be sure to browse our roundup of the Absolute Best Prime Day Deals.Updated 7 am ET, June 24: We've added the Samsung S90F QD-OLED TV, Apple AirPods Max 2, Apple Watch Series 11, and the EcoFlow River 2 Pro.Best Tech DealsPhotograph: Ryan WaniataSamsung S90F QD-OLED TV for $1,198 ($500 off)The best TV for gaming is incredibly accurate, offers near-infinite contrast, and boasts vibrant yet refined colors. Samsung's gaming hub is the best around whether you're playing on a console or cloud gaming, and features an intuitive gaming bar, snappy input response, and a full suite of HDMI 2.1 ports.TCL QM6K QLED TV (65-inch) for $528 ($70 off)Affordable at its regular price, the TCL QM6K is a steal with this discount. The 65-inch model employs QLED display tech for bright colors and good contrast, even from a side viewing angle. Gamers will appreciate the 144 Hz refresh rate for low-latency performance, all powered by the intuitive Google TV operating system.Roku Streaming Stick 4K TV Streamer for $44 ($5 off)The best streaming device for most folks, this Roku stick is small, fast, and plugs right into your TV's HDMI port. It's super simple to use and has a handy remote for voice search support.Photograph: Ryan WaniataJBL Flip 7 Bluetooth Speaker for $95 ($55 off)Our favorite portable Bluetooth speaker, JBL’s Flip strikes the perfect balance between mobility and sound quality. The Flip 7 also has some extra goodies like water resistance, an easy-release strap, and a two-hour battery boost (or four with reduced bass).Beats Powerbeats Pro 2 Wireless Earbuds for $180 ($69 off)With the same H2 chip that powers the AirPods Pro 2, the PowerBeats Pro 2 impressed our reviewer, who praised the “crazy comfy” fit and dynamic sound. There’s also ANC and transparency modes for both road safety and gym sanity, wireless charging, IP57 durability, full-on bass-forward audio quality, and built-in heart rate monitoring.Apple AirPods Max 2 Headphones for $399 ($150 off)With the same gorgeous, comfortable, iconic design as its predecessor, Apple’s AirPods Max 2 bring improved noise canceling and Bluetooth connectivity. Apple also upgraded software features, like gesture controls and Adaptive Audio. They’re a bit pricey at the RRP, but this discount is decent for Apple fans.Photograph: Adrienne SoApple Watch Series 11 Smartwatch for $279 ($120 off)The Apple Watch Series 11 is by far the best smartwatch for iPhone owners. The headline improvement is 24-hour battery life, which enables proper sleep tracking. You also get clinically validated watchOS health updates, including FDA-cleared hypertension notifications, Apple’s proprietary Sleep Score, and blood oxygen sensing.Apple 11-inch iPad (2025) for $299 ($50 off)The base iPad ticks all the boxes you need in a tablet. This is the latest model, updated in 2025 to an A16 chip and 6 GB of RAM. It's perfect for casual gaming, reading books, watching movies in bed, and sharing with your kids. Amazon has the same sale for Prime members right now, but this is as good a deal as we typically see on iPads.Apple AirTag Tracker for $24 ($5 off)These teeny tiny trackers might be the best way to keep tabs on your keys or luggage if you use an iPhone. They're some of the best trackers around. Roughly the size of a quarter, they use Bluetooth and Apple's special U1 location-finding chip to pinpoint locations in the Find My app.Photograph: Simon HillNex Playground Game Console for $239 ($60 off)Worried about your kids becoming couch potatoes? This fun game console will get them up and jumping around your living room. I liked the Nex Playground when I first tried it, and the company has polished and added more games since then.Microsoft Xbox Series X Game Console for $573 ($76 off)It might be late in this console generation's lifespan, but you can still snag Microsoft's flagship Xbox Series X, and it might be the best way to take full advantage of its Game Pass library. It's not as good a deal as it was, but what is nowadays?EcoFlow River 2 Pro Portable Power Station for $290 ($240 off)This is one of the best portable power stations for camping or road trips because it’s a manageable size. EcoFlow’s River 2 Pro has a LiFeP04 battery inside, good for 768 watt-hours. It has all the ports you need to charge your phones and small gadgets, and the X-Boost surge enables it to power small appliances.Photograph: Matthew KorfhageNinja Slushi Machine for $179 ($120 off)Just in time for the summer sun, the best slushie machine is on sale. Whip up icy soft drinks for the kids and a frozen margarita for you. The perfect way to cool down on hot days, this machine has intuitive controls and a reliable drip tray, and it's easy to clean.Ninja Luxe Cafe Pro Espresso Machine for $600 ($150 off)Ninja’s flagship semi-automatic espresso machine can go toe-to-toe with Breville’s Barista Express for the title of most popular semi-automatic espresso machine. This is a great deal on a machine that nails automatic milk frothing.Google Fitbit Charge 6 Fitness Tracker for $76 ($84 off)The Charge 6 is old, but it's still our favorite Fitbit and may remain so until the Fitbit name is submerged by Google. The bright AMOLED touchscreen is easy to read and it covers all the basics, monitoring your heart rate, blood oxygen, and skin temperature. It also has ECG and electrodermal activity scans to gauge irregular heart rhythms and stress levels.Petlibro Scout Smart Pet Camera for $56 ($46 off)Keep an eye on your pets with this AI-enabled smart pet camera. It features 1080p HD video, black-and-white or color night vision, and two-way audio. The built-in AI learns who your pets are, and uploads photos that you can review to help it get smarter.Google Nest Cam Outdoor for $130 ($50 off)The best wireless outdoor security camera for Google households, the Nest Cam Outdoor has a handy magnetic mount, an expansive 130-degree field of view, and captures sharp 1080p video with HDR and night vision. There's also a clear-sounding speaker and microphone. You only get three hours of free event history, but you can add a Home Premium subscription ($10 a month, or $100 for the whole year) to get 30 days of event history and face recognition.Bissell Little Green Portable Carpet Cleaner for $85 ($45 off)You may remember this gadget went viral on TikTok a few years ago, and it's still an excellent upholstery or carpet cleaner at its regular price. It's pretty good at sucking up spills and may even remove stains, and it's way less bulky than your average carpet cleaner.Most PopularGearThe 129 Absolute Best Prime Day DealsBy Louryn StrampeHow To and Advice28 Tips to Take Your ChatGPT Prompts to the Next LevelBy David NieldGearThe Best Prime Day Tech Deals on the Gadgets You Actually WantBy Simon HillGearThe 15 Best Apple Deals to Snag During Amazon Prime DayBy Louryn Strampe
